pratt.title: DESIGN Climate & Clean-Energy Engineering
pratt.desc: Pratt is one of Duke's most prolific contributors to the Climate Commitment. DESIGN Climate deploys interdisciplinary student-faculty teams on real client climate challenges across two project-based courses. The new 18-month Master of Engineering in Climate & Sustainability Engineering covers energy transition, sustainable infrastructure, sustainable materials, and climate finance. Faculty like Liang Feng lead cutting-edge carbon-capture research converting plastic waste into CO₂ adsorbents, and the Gendell Center for Engineering, Energy, and the Environment trains undergraduates on real energy systems from molecules to megawatts.

sanford.title: Climate Policy, Health & Environmental Justice
sanford.desc: Ranked #2 in energy and environmental policy, Sanford shapes climate narratives and advises policymakers at every level. Former EPA Administrator Michael Regan joins as a Polis Distinguished Fellow. Faculty testify before Congress, advise international climate negotiations, and lead research spanning energy transition governance, environmental racism in North Carolina, climate health equity, and food systems resilience. Sanford's World Food Policy Center and Duke Center for International Development extend this work globally.

scales.title: SCALES, SCALe & Climate Scholar Programs
scales.desc: Duke's cross-university climate programs create a web of climate engagement touching every school. SCALES will support 24 postdoctoral fellows across 6 cohorts (2026–2033). SCALe runs applied learning projects using Duke's campus as a living lab, offering mini grants, faculty partnerships, and Green Devil internships. Climate Scholars, Climate+, and Bass Connections provide students at every level with hands-on research experience, while the Climate and Sustainability Teaching Fellows embed climate content across disciplines.

athletics.title: Athletics Climate Team: Leaders On and Off the Field
athletics.desc: Duke Athletics launched a 40-member Athletics Climate Team (ACT) bringing together student-athletes, coaches, and staff across every sport. Imani Dorsey — a former All-America women's soccer player — returned to Duke specifically as the Athletics Climate & Sustainability Coordinator. Duke hosted the inaugural Collegiate Sports Sustainability Network, bringing together 75+ leaders from 50+ universities. Climate Commitment Games, uniform recycling, LED retrofits at Cameron Indoor, and the ReBounce tennis ball program make Duke Athletics a measurable force for sustainability.

nicholas.title: Climate Research & Environmental Leadership
nicholas.desc: The Nicholas School is central to Duke's Climate Commitment across every dimension — education, research, operations, external engagement, and community partnerships. The 2025 annual report spotlights Nicholas faculty working across five priority areas. A new joint Master in Business, Climate & Sustainability with Fuqua, a dedicated institutional Climate Action Plan, four decades of environmental justice scholarship, and a strong presence in the Climate Research Innovation Seed Program all make Nicholas the cornerstone of Duke's climate ecosystem.

nicholas-institute.school: Nicholas Institute for Energy, Environment & Sustainability
nicholas-institute.title: Climate TRACE, Heat Policy & Data-Driven Solutions
nicholas-institute.desc: The Nicholas Institute operates at the intersection of data and climate policy. Climate TRACE — now presenting potential solutions at COP30 — tracks greenhouse gas emissions at source level across every major sector worldwide using satellite imagery and machine learning. The Heat Policy Innovation Hub, the first of its kind in the U.S., develops cross-disciplinary heat policy. The Institute also coordinates 12 interdisciplinary climate research teams, runs the State Policy Program, and provides students hands-on access to international climate diplomacy through the U.N. Negotiations Practicum.

operations.title: Sustainable Campus Operations
operations.desc: Duke achieved carbon neutrality in FY2024 — the first large research university on the East Coast to reach this milestone, honoring a pledge made in 2007. A 101 MW solar partnership with Pine Gate Renewables will supply 50% of campus electricity by mid-2025. Duke's campus has expanded substantially while cutting absolute emissions. The Operations subcommittee is now developing a 2050 net-zero target that for the first time encompasses the entire Duke University Health System.

marine.title: Oceans@Duke & Coastal Resilience
marine.desc: The Duke Marine Lab — operating for over 85 years from its Beaufort, NC base — places scientists at the frontline of ocean acidification, salt marsh restoration, whale monitoring, and coastal community resilience. Oceans Week 2025 convened students, faculty, and professionals around AI in marine science and plastic pollution. The Marine Geospatial Ecology Lab applies cutting-edge spatial analysis to climate and biodiversity. The Ready, Set, Resilience K–12 program connects coastal school children to climate science through storytelling and hands-on curriculum.

fuqua.title: ClimateCAP & MBA Sustainability Program
fuqua.desc: Fuqua is Duke's bridge between climate science and business strategy. The EDGE Center leads ClimateCAP — a 48-school global initiative that reached 3,000+ MBA students in 2024–25 and launched ClimateCATALYST for 500+ incoming MBA students. The new joint Master in Business, Climate & Sustainability with the Nicholas School launches July 2026. Fuqua also offers an Energy Finance concentration, an ESG resources center, and dual MEM/MBA and MF/MBA degrees.

chemistry.title: Next-Generation Renewable Energy Research
chemistry.desc: Five Duke Chemistry faculty — Beratan, Liu, Moreno-Hernandez, Therien, and Wiley — are funded through the Climate Research Innovation Seed Program to advance renewable energy science. Therien's three-decade DOE-funded program uses plant leaves as a guide for bio-inspired solar harvesting. Beratan and Therien lead an NSF Center for Synthesizing Quantum Coherence. Wiley develops low-cost microfiber electrodes for green hydrogen. Liu converts solar energy into transportable hydrogen fuels. Moreno-Hernandez's NSF CAREER work advances electrocatalysis for renewable chemical production.
